commands.review_summary:
The PR adjusts one Antigravity HTTPS readiness-error test so it asserts the final error message using the observed attempt count instead of requiring multiple polling attempts.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not from an executed test in this read-only review. With a short deadline and blocking fetch, waitForSnapshot can legally throw the latest fetch error after a single attempt if the deadline is exhausted.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Hold for contributor proof, CI evidence, or maintainer proof override; there is no code repair for ClawSweeper to queue.

target.title: test: stabilize Antigravity readiness error assertion
target.body:
## Summary
- keep the Antigravity readiness-error test focused on preserving the latest fetch error
- avoid requiring more than one wall-clock polling attempt in CI

## Context
The retry behavior is already covered by separate Antigravity tests. This assertion can fail on faster CI/test-runtime combinations when the wait exits after a single fetch attempt, while still returning the latest readiness error correctly.

## Test plan
- `arch -x86_64 swift test --scratch-path .build/x86-antigravity-separate-pr-check --filter AntigravityCLIHTTPSFetchStrategyTests --skip-update`
target.author: hololee
